Title: A Genetic Screen Reveals Arabidopsis Stomatal and/or Apoplastic Defenses against Pseudomonas syringae pv. tomato DC3000

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ABC-F family ATP-binding cassette protein; AtGCN20/AtABCF3; nucleotide-binding domains without transmembrane domains
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports defense-associated stomatal closure restricting bacterial entry
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SCORD5 is required for bacterium- and MAMP-triggered stomatal closure, but not for SA- or ABA-induced stomatal closure; loss of SCORD5 increases susceptibility to surface-inoculated COR-deficient Pst DC3118
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA insertion mapping; RT-PCR loss-of-transcript confirmation; independent knockout allele (scord5-2/188G03); genomic complementation; stomatal aperture assays; bacterial growth assays
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SCORD5 was cloned as AtGCN20/AtABCF3. 2. Two independent knockout alleles failed to close stomata in response to Pst DC3118, flg22, and elf18. 3. Genomic complementation restored bacterium-triggered stomatal closure. 4. SCORD5 acts upstream of SA/ABA-triggered closure because SA and ABA responses remained normal.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native genomic fragment with ~480 bp upstream region used for complementation
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: ILA (genetic/functional association inferred from GCN1/GCN20-related pathway)
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutants showed slightly smaller rosettes and pale green leaves
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to improve stomatal immunity against bacterial entry, though pleiotropic growth/color effects should be monitored
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pst DC3118 infection; flg22 and elf18 treatment; SA and ABA treatment
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Predicted to function in stress-associated protein translation control rather than direct metabolism; identified as a novel regulator of stomatal defense relevant to SA-associated immune metabolite action

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Associated with immune stomatal closure restricting bacterial entry
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ILA is required for bacterium-triggered stomatal closure, supporting a GCN1/GCN20-associated translation control mechanism in stomatal defense
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant stomatal aperture assay using ila-3 response to Pst DC3118
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ila-3 stomata were unresponsive to Pst DC3118. 2. This supports involvement of a GCN1/GCN20-like translation control mechanism in bacterium-triggered stomatal closure.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: ila-3 plants were reported as slightly smaller than scord5 plants
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for improving stomatal defense, but likely with broader defense/growth consequences
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not cloned in this study but experimentally tested in Results as part of the SCORD5-associated pathway; involvement is in defense-associated translation control rather than direct metabolite biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: MATE-family transporter; putative salicylic acid biosynthesis-related transporter
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Does not catalyze a chemical conversion; required for salicylic acid biosynthesis/accumulation as a transporter-associated step
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for biosynthesis of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling required for bacterium-triggered stomatal closure and apoplastic defense against Pseudomonas syringae
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of EDS5 strongly reduces basal and bacterium-induced salicylic acid accumulation and compromises bacterium-triggered stomatal closure, increasing susceptibility to coronatine-deficient Pst DC3118
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant identification by T-DNA mapping; allelism test with eds5-1; salicylic acid quantification by LC/MS; stomatal aperture assays; bacterial growth assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SCORD3 was cloned as EDS5. 2. The scord3/eds5-4 mutant had nearly abolished basal and induced salicylic acid accumulation. 3. Loss of EDS5 impaired bacterium-triggered stomatal closure and increased susceptibility to Pst DC3118.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for salicylic acid accumulation in this study, but rate-limiting status not directly established
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Maintaining or enhancing EDS5 function could support salicylic acid-associated disease resistance traits
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pst DC3000 or COR-deficient Pst DC3118 infection; SA treatment in stomatal assays
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although EDS5 is a transporter rather than a catalytic enzyme, it is directly required for accumulation of the nutrient-related defense metabolite salicylic acid and was a cloned core gene in the Results
